# FileUpload

A file upload component with dropzone, trigger button, and file item display. Composed of multiple sub-components for flexible layout.

## Import

```jsx
import {
  FileUpload,
  FileUploadDropzone,
  FileUploadTrigger,
  FileUploadLabel,
  FileUploadItemGroup,
  FileUploadItem,
  FileUploadItemPreview,
  FileUploadItemName,
  FileUploadItemSizeText,
  FileUploadItemDeleteTrigger,
} from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";
```

## Sub-components

| Component | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `FileUpload` | The root wrapper (renders `FileUpload.Root`). Manages file state |
| `FileUploadDropzone` | The drag-and-drop zone for files |
| `FileUploadTrigger` | A clickable trigger to open the file dialog |
| `FileUploadLabel` | A label for the upload area |
| `FileUploadItemGroup` | Container for the list of uploaded files |
| `FileUploadItem` | An individual uploaded file entry |
| `FileUploadItemPreview` | Preview of the uploaded file (e.g., image thumbnail) |
| `FileUploadItemName` | Displays the file name |
| `FileUploadItemSizeText` | Displays the file size |
| `FileUploadItemDeleteTrigger` | Button to remove an uploaded file |

## FileUpload (Root) Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `accept` | `string \| string[]` | - | Accepted file types (e.g., `"image/*"`, `".pdf"`) |
| `maxFiles` | `number` | - | Maximum number of files allowed |
| `maxFileSize` | `number` | - | Maximum file size in bytes |
| `onFileAccept` | `(details: { files: File[] }) => void` | - | Callback fired when files are accepted |
| `onFileReject` | `(details: { files: FileRejection[] }) => void` | - | Callback fired when files are rejected |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Whether the upload is disabled |
| `ref` | `React.Ref` | - | Forwarded ref to the root element |

## Usage

### Basic with dropzone

```jsx
<FileUpload accept="image/*" maxFiles={3}>
  <FileUploadDropzone>
    <FileUploadLabel>Drag and drop files here</FileUploadLabel>
    <FileUploadTrigger>Choose Files</FileUploadTrigger>
  </FileUploadDropzone>
  <FileUploadItemGroup>
    <FileUploadItem>
      <FileUploadItemPreview />
      <FileUploadItemName />
      <FileUploadItemSizeText />
      <FileUploadItemDeleteTrigger />
    </FileUploadItem>
  </FileUploadItemGroup>
</FileUpload>
```

### Button trigger only

```jsx
<FileUpload accept=".pdf,.doc,.docx">
  <FileUploadTrigger>Upload Document</FileUploadTrigger>
  <FileUploadItemGroup>
    <FileUploadItem>
      <FileUploadItemName />
      <FileUploadItemDeleteTrigger />
    </FileUploadItem>
  </FileUploadItemGroup>
</FileUpload>
```

### With file size limit

```jsx
<FileUpload
  maxFileSize={5 * 1024 * 1024}
  onFileReject={(details) => console.log("Rejected:", details.files)}
>
  <FileUploadDropzone>
    <FileUploadLabel>Max file size: 5MB</FileUploadLabel>
    <FileUploadTrigger>Browse</FileUploadTrigger>
  </FileUploadDropzone>
</FileUpload>
```

### With callbacks

```jsx
<FileUpload
  accept="image/*"
  maxFiles={5}
  onFileAccept={(details) => console.log("Accepted:", details.files)}
  onFileReject={(details) => console.log("Rejected:", details.files)}
>
  <FileUploadDropzone>
    <FileUploadTrigger>Upload Images</FileUploadTrigger>
  </FileUploadDropzone>
</FileUpload>
```

## Features

- Compound component pattern for fully customizable upload layouts
- Drag-and-drop file uploading via the dropzone
- File type filtering with the `accept` prop
- File count and size limits with rejection callbacks
- File item display with preview, name, size, and delete functionality
- Each sub-component forwards refs to its underlying DOM element
